items.hook: “Locarno is the place to see how a film can work,” says festival director Giona A Nazzaro
items.body: Nazzaro talks about his love for genre filmmaking and US cinema and embracing political debate.

items.hook: Akashic Records of Bastard Magic Instructor is a Japanese light novel series written by Tarō Hitsuji and illustrated by Kurone Mishima.
items.body: Fujimi Shobo has published twenty-four volumes from July 19, 2014, to November 17, 2023, under their Fujimi Fantasia Bunko imprint. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The 79th Locarno Film Festival unveils the members of its juries - Locarno 2026
items.body: Fabrice Du Welz, Marco Alessi, Lolita Chammah, Paulina García and Olivier Père will serve on the International Competition jury

items.hook: The List of Adrian Messenger is a 1963 American mystery film directed by John Huston starring Kirk Douglas, George C.
items.body: Scott, Dana Wynter, Clive Brook, Gladys Cooper and Herbert Marshall. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Jean-Baptiste Durand’s L’homme qui avait peur des femmes receives the CNC’s advance on receipts - Production / Funding - France
items.body: The French organisation will also back the next films by Yolande Zauberman, Baya Kasmi, Cécile Ducrocq, Marya Zarif and Ilan Klipper

items.hook: Drug discovery Is changing. Drug development must change too.
items.body: In this New Scientist CoLab podcast, experts from global life sciences leader Cytiva explain the hidden, high-stakes science of purification that is required to close the gap between drug discovery and the pharmacy shelf.

items.hook: It is largely a residential area with several commercial areas, a Recreation Centre with a hockey area and curling rink.
items.body: Brookswood is a community in the Township of Langley, British Columbia, Canada, located on the west side of that municipality to the south of the Nicomekl River. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Armadale is a novel by Wilkie Collins, first serialised between November 1864 and June 1866, and then published in book form in 1866.
items.body: It is the third of his four 'great novels' of the 1860s: after The Woman in White (1860) and No Name (1862), and before The Moonstone (1868). Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Mysteries of London is a "penny blood" or city mysteries novel begun by George W.

items.body:

items.body:

items.hook: MUBI and BIM join forces on cinema distribution in Italy - Distribution / Releases / Exhibitors - Italy
items.body: The movie theatre partnership signed between BIM Distribuzione and MUBI Italia under the aegis of Gabriele D’Andrea, will come into effect on 1 August, bringing arthouse films to cinemas

items.hook: By Moonlight is a 1958 novel by British SOE officer Peter Churchill, DSO, Croix de Guerre.
items.body: It is based on the true story of the Royal Air Force supplying arms and supplies to the Maquis des Glières, who were the first French resistance group to fight a pitched battle against the Germans in World War II. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
